content: Just as the language-learning definition of reflexion frames it as a process of mirroring external input to refine one's own expressive output, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this framing to introduce graded, language pedagogy-style reflexion routines that improve grounded reasoning. This treats self-reflection not as an emergent abstract capability, but as a learnable skill that can be trained through structured repeated practice like how human students learn to reflective on their own writing.
